---
title: "nitrix.morphology.pooling"
generated: true
signature-hash: 86893c62e59f2d0a
description: "Encoder-decoder pooling primitives with index-bookkeeping."
listing:
  id: nitrix-morphology-pooling-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-morphology-pooling/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.morphology.pooling`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Encoder-decoder pooling primitives with index-bookkeeping.

[`max_pool_with_indices_nd`](nitrix-morphology-pooling/max_pool_with_indices_nd.qmd) records the per-window argmax
position during the pool, returning *both* the pooled values and
the per-output integer indices.  [`max_unpool_nd`](nitrix-morphology-pooling/max_unpool_nd.qmd) scatters
values back into a higher-resolution grid at those same positions.
The pair is what an "indices-aware" U-Net / V-Net encoder-decoder
uses to preserve spatial localisation across the bottleneck.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:__module__ -->
### Family

These belong with [`dilate`](nitrix-morphology/dilate.qmd) and [`erode`](nitrix-morphology/erode.qmd) in
`nitrix.morphology`: both [`max_pool_with_indices_nd`](nitrix-morphology-pooling/max_pool_with_indices_nd.qmd) and
[`dilate`](nitrix-morphology/dilate.qmd) are max-window reductions; the only difference is
that pooling **strides** the window (one output per stride'd
position) while dilation keeps the spatial shape.

### Channel first layout

PyTorch / FreeSurfer-Keras / Flax-LayerNorm convention; channel
axis precedes the spatial axes (`(*batch, C, *spatial)`).  The
caller specifies the spatial rank explicitly via `spatial_rank`
to disambiguate batch vs channel; we don't infer.

### Cross framework parity caveat

The PGlandsSeg port flagged this and the docstring repeats it
explicitly: argmax-based pooling is **fragile to cross-framework
float noise**.  When the encoder accumulates ~1e-3 max abs
difference between JAX and torch (typical for a 4-level Conv3D
cascade), the per-window argmax can flip at ~0.02-0.03% of voxels
where two neighbours are nearly equal.  The matching
[`max_unpool_nd`](nitrix-morphology-pooling/max_unpool_nd.qmd) then scatters values to slightly different
positions, producing O(10)
per-voxel raw-logit differences.  At the semantic level (per-voxel
class via argmax over channel axis) this is harmless -- the
inter-class ordering is preserved.

**The load-bearing parity check is argmax-of-output agreement,
not raw-logit allclose.**  When validating against a torch or TF
reference, compare `argmax(out, channel_axis)`, not
`allclose(out, ref)`.

### Reference

Badrinarayanan, V., Kendall, A. & Cipolla, R. (2017).  *SegNet: A
deep convolutional encoder-decoder architecture for image
segmentation.*  IEEE TPAMI 39(12), 2481-2495.  The original
indices-based encoder-decoder pattern.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `*batch` | leading batch axes (variadic) |
| `C` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
| `*pooled` | a variadic group of axes (`pooled`) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-morphology-pooling-symbols}
:::
